headline: An AI-assisted hunt unearths Squidbleed, a 29-year-old Squid proxy flaw, the same day stolen Klue OAuth tokens spread to HackerOne, Recorded Future, Snyk, and Tanium.

emerging_trends.text: Researchers increasingly turn large language models loose on aging code, and the Squidbleed disclosure—a 29-year-old heap over-read surfaced with Claude Mythos Preview—shows the technique flushing out flaws that sat untouched for decades.

Attackers keep trading malware and zero-days for stolen credentials, and the Klue token theft now bleeding into HackerOne, Recorded Future, Snyk, and Tanium proves how far one set of pilfered tokens travels.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Klue Hack Pulls In Seven More Security Firms

emerging_trends.text: The rush to bolt AI agents into developer workflows keeps opening fresh holes, from 23 ClawHub plugins squatting official scopes to 282 iOS apps spilling backend credentials.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AI Agent Plugins and Apps Spill Secrets

topics.title: Squidbleed Exposes 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Flaw
topics.latest_developments: Researchers at Calif.io disclosed Squidbleed, a heap over-read tracing to a 1997 FTP-parsing change in the Squid web proxy that they surfaced with help from Claude Mythos Preview, and SecurityWeek likened it to Heartbleed.
topics.summary: The over-read leaks another user's cleartext HTTP request, including credentials or session tokens, to anyone the proxy already permits, and it survives in Squid's default configuration. Operators running Squid should patch at once.

sources.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw 'Squidbleed' Can Expose User Data

topics.title: AI Agent Plugins and Apps Spill Secrets
topics.latest_developments: Manifold Security's Ax Sharma found 23 code-executing plugins squatting ClawHub's official @openclaw and @clawhub scopes, and Wake Forest University researchers found 282 of 444 AI-powered iOS apps exposing exploitable LLM credentials or backend access.
topics.summary: AI plugin registries and mobile apps hand attackers both running code and stolen keys: ClawHub failed to reserve its official scopes, and hundreds of iOS apps embed LLM API credentials an interceptor can lift. Developers should pin trusted publishers and move secrets server-side.

sources.title: 23 ClawHub plugins squatting official scopes expose AI registry security gaps
Hundreds of AI-powered iOS apps found exposing credentials

topics.title: AI Sharpens Identity Fraud and World Cup Scams
topics.latest_developments: Israeli investigators described a fraudster who used AI to generate face-swap videos impersonating victims and draining their money, and Wired reported AI magnifying World Cup ticket and cloned-website scams ahead of the tournament.
topics.summary: Generative AI now produces convincing face-swap clips and fake event sites at scale, raising the bar for spotting fraud. Consumers should verify ticket sellers and treat unexpected video proof with suspicion.

sources.title: He Used AI Agents To Wear Other People's Faces And Steal Money
World Cup Scams Are Getting Harder to Spot

topics.title: Klue Hack Pulls In Seven More Security Firms
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek named seven more victims—HackerOne, Huntress, Jamf, OneTrust, Recorded Future, Snyk, and Tanium—widening the fallout from the Klue OAuth-token theft.
topics.summary: Sales-intelligence vendor Klue lost OAuth tokens that linked to customers' Salesforce environments, and the latest disclosures pull a roster of major security firms into the blast radius. Affected customers should revoke tokens and audit Salesforce activity.

sources.title: More Cybersecurity Firms Disclose Impact From Klue Hack

topics.title: OXLOADER Delivers CastleStealer Through Google Ads
topics.latest_developments: Elastic Security Labs disclosed OXLOADER, a previously unreported loader that buys malicious Google Ads to deliver the CastleStealer infostealer, tying the operator to a Russian-speaking, financially motivated crew.
topics.summary: OXLOADER starts from poisoned search ads, then drops CastleStealer to harvest data from infected machines. Defenders should block malvertising and scrutinize software downloaded through ad clicks.

sources.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer

topics.title: Canada's CSIS Cleans Botnets Under Novel Warrant
topics.latest_developments: The Federal Court released on June 15 a public version of a ruling showing the Canadian Security Intelligence Service used a threat reduction warrant for the first time to reach into infected Canadian servers, home routers, and IoT gear and neutralize two foreign-run botnets.
topics.summary: A judge let CSIS alter compromised devices on Canadian soil to dismantle the botnets, the first use of these warrant powers for that purpose. The ruling sets a precedent for intelligence agencies remediating infections directly.

sources.title: Canada's Spy Agency Used First-of-Its-Kind Warrant to Clean Botnet-Infected Devices

weather: This Afternoon: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely, high 78F.
Tonight: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely then Mostly Cloudy, low 59F.

games.recap: Gonzalez and Reynolds homer as the Pirates hold off the Rockies 8-6

business_politics.title: Starmer Resigns as UK Prime Minister
business_politics.latest_developments: Keir Starmer announced Monday he will leave Downing Street within weeks, ending the speculation reported earlier, with Health Secretary Wes Streeting backing former Manchester mayor Andy Burnham to succeed him.
business_politics.summary: Starmer quit as Labour leader and prime minister roughly two years after a landslide win, undone by a rebellion after May's local-election rout; Britain now heads for its sixth prime minister in seven years, and sterling and gilts steadied after the announcement.

sources.title: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer Quits After Party Rebellion
Keir Starmer steps down as UK prime minister

business_politics.title: Iran Agrees to Readmit Nuclear Inspectors
business_politics.latest_developments: Vice President JD Vance said Monday that Iran agreed to invite International Atomic Energy Agency inspectors back in, calling it a 'major milestone' as the Switzerland talks continued.
business_politics.summary: U.S. and Iranian negotiators in Switzerland worked toward a permanent deal to end a four-month conflict; oil eased and over 400 tankers wait near the Strait of Hormuz, where transit is creeping back but could take weeks to reach half of prewar levels.

sources.title: Vance Says Iran Agreed to Allow Nuclear Inspectors Back In
Over 400 ships are waiting near Hormuz for the strait to reopen fully

business.title: PNC Closes $251 Million Affordable-Housing Fund
business.latest_developments: PNC Bank announced Monday it closed a $251.4 million fund to develop and preserve affordable rental housing across several states.
business.summary: The Pittsburgh-based bank's fund targets construction and preservation of affordable rental units nationwide, channeling institutional capital into a housing supply squeeze.

sources.title: PNC Bank closes $251 million fund to develop affordable housing across multiple states

business.title: Pittsburgh Pitches Itself as Defense-Tech Hub
business.latest_developments: The Army AI Integration Center showcased military robotics and artificial intelligence at Carnegie Mellon University's Robotics, AI and Autonomy Forum.
business.summary: Carnegie Mellon, a longtime Army partner, hosted the forum to position Pittsburgh as a center for defense robotics and AI and to identify the military's critical needs.

sources.title: Pittsburgh positions itself as defense tech hub at CMU robotics forum

business.title: Pitt and JMI Sports Launch H2PNIL Platform
business.latest_developments: Pitt Athletics and JMI Sports launched H2PNIL, a platform to drive name-image-likeness revenue and serve as a recruiting tool for coaches.
business.summary: The platform expands the existing Pitt-JMI partnership, aiming to grow NIL dollars for University of Pittsburgh athletes and sharpen the school's competitive recruiting pitch.

sources.title: Pitt Athletics, JMI Sports launch new platform to elevate NIL support

around_town.title: Parkway East Bridge to Come Down by Controlled Blast
around_town.latest_developments: WTAE detailed the full timeline of PennDOT's demolition on the Interstate 376 Parkway East, with crews working before and after a controlled blast to drop the old span.
around_town.summary: PennDOT will use a controlled blast to bring down the aging bridge over the Parkway East, with demolition phases bracketing the explosion and affecting one of Pittsburgh's busiest commuter routes.

sources.title: Get the Facts: What is the total duration of demolition work on Parkway East for 376 Bridge Closure?

around_town.title: Tornado Threat Added to Monday Storms
around_town.latest_developments: The severe-weather threat shifted northward over Pittsburgh, prompting a First Alert Weather Day with damaging winds and possible tornadoes Monday afternoon, June 22, beyond the soaking-rain forecast reported earlier.
around_town.summary: Forecasters warned of isolated heavy downpours, gusty thunderstorms, and a tornado risk across western Pennsylvania on Monday, with a renewed chance of storms returning Thursday.

sources.title: Damaging winds, tornadoes could hit Pittsburgh area on Monday
Impact Day: Flooding and strong storm threat this afternoon

events.title: Pittsburgh Symphony's Summer of Movie Music
events.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette previewed the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra's summer slate built around film scores.
events.summary: The Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra is devoting its summer season to movie music, performing scores from the screen for Pittsburgh audiences.

sources.title: The Pittsburgh Symphony's summer of movie music

around_teams.title: McCarthy Wants a 3-4 Defense in Pittsburgh
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported that Mike McCarthy, who won a Super Bowl with a 3-4 defense, aims to build the Steelers' scheme in that mold under coordinator Patrick Graham.
around_teams.summary: McCarthy pointed to the lineage of Bill Cowher, Dick LeBeau, and Dom Capers as he laid out a 3-4 vision for the Steelers' defense heading into 2026.

sources.title: Mike McCarthy won a Super Bowl with a 3-4 defense. He wants to emulate it with the Steelers.

around_teams.title: DK Metcalf's Year 2 Upside
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette podcast weighed how much more receiver DK Metcalf can give the Steelers in his second season with the team.
around_teams.summary: The discussion gauged Metcalf's ceiling in 2026 as the Steelers' top wideout after his first year in Pittsburgh.

sources.title: WATCH: How much upside does DK Metcalf have in Year 2 with the Steelers?

around_teams.title: Pirates Prospect Wietgrefe Riding a Scoreless Streak
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette's MiLB Monday highlighted pitching prospect Connor Wietgrefe's scoreless streak at Double-A Altoona.
around_teams.summary: Wietgrefe has drawn notice in the Pirates' farm system with a run of shutout work at Altoona, one of several prospects the column tracked across the minor leagues.

sources.title: MiLB Monday: Pirates prospect Connor Wietgrefe is turning heads with his scoreless streak in Altoona

team_usa.title: USMNT Weighs Rotation Before Türkiye Finale
team_usa.latest_developments: With the U.S. men's national team already through to the knockout round, the question turned to whether coaches rotate the squad or keep the same starting eleven for the final Group D match against Türkiye.
team_usa.summary: The USMNT clinched a place in the World Cup round of 32 as a co-host and now faces a lineup decision for its closing group game against Türkiye, balancing rest against momentum.

sources.title: USMNT's World Cup success brings dilemma: Rotate the squad or keep the same XI

team_usa.title: Alex Freeman's Path to the USMNT
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN detailed how Alex Freeman, son of former Green Bay Packers Pro Bowl receiver Antonio Freeman, reached the U.S. men's World Cup roster.
team_usa.summary: Freeman, raised by an NFL father, carved his own route into the USMNT and is now playing in the 2026 World Cup on home soil.

sources.title: How Freeman's Pro Bowl dad helped him live his World Cup dream

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport argues that AI companies undercut their own credibility by publicizing alarmist warnings about their products, likening it to a carmaker raising doom about its own bestselling vehicle.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Apple Price Increases, Apple Intelligence and the E.U.
reading.summary: Ben Thompson examines Apple finally raising prices while withholding its Siri AI features from the European Union, tying the move to regulatory friction.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2)
reading.summary: Zitron extends his case that Silicon Valley is in a bubble, drawing on OpenAI's audited financials showing $34 billion in spending against $13.07 billion in revenue.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
